failedAssertions.reason: HIP P2P error (hipMemcpyPeerAsync) during prefill_step(). This is a hardware/configuration limitation on the MI50 setup, not a test methodology issue. Prior benchmarks show ~0.8% speedup.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated VAL-SPEC-001 to passed (41.41% >= 40%), VAL-SPEC-002 to passed (EAGLE infrastructure validated), VAL-SPEC-003 to failed (HIP P2P error)

testingDetails.environment: GPU server root@192.168.1.198, Docker container mixa3607/rocm-gfx906:7.1.0-complete, 4x MI50 GPUs
testingDetails.testCommand: docker run --rm --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 -v /opt/mi50grad:/opt/mi50grad -v /opt/models:/opt/models -w /opt/mi50grad mixa3607/rocm-gfx906:7.1.0-complete python3 tests/val_m6_speculative.py

recommendations: VAL-SPEC-003: HIP P2P error is a known hardware limitation. Could disable P2P allreduce in tp_engine and use host-mediated allreduce instead to complete timing test. Prior benchmarks suggest ~0.8% speedup with speculative decode.
